reviews.review_msg: This guy is great! Funny, interesting material, and well worth rocking up to the 8.30 lectures!!

It does not seem like Dr. Ley prepares for his lectures, which is very off-putting when you are paying for a class.  I wanted to work with adolescents, so it was extremely important for me to get a good grade in his class.  However, he was rude and sarcastic when I asked questions, and spoke more of anecdotal experience.  The tests were irrelevant.
know the text. if you miss lecture, you didnt miss much.
how can you entertain, my classmate. Cant believe this is an Econ course. Super boring and useless lecture

Chris was very helpful and I appreciated his sense of humour. With that said, be sure to pay attention to lectures... if you do not, then do not expect him to give you all the answers. If you show you care, he will too. This class was the most practical class I have taken in my sociology degree thus far!

The Good:
- Responsive over email and office hours
- Knowledgeable in the field

The Bad:
- Exams are worth 55% when the course is almost predominantly cookbook-style labs, so exams are the first time you apply your knowledge
- Frustrating marking in labs

The lab is totally runned by TA Ashley this semester. lab guidance is always confusing and not detailed enough to make sense. (Especially the Discover QGIS book) Some important scoring points are not labeled in the assignment introduction, if you explain scoring points in lab then the same you should write them in canvas.
she's not helpful. sometimes rude to students. anal. lectures are disorganized.
